title: Lynx get Collier back from double ankle surgeries, on minutes restriction
dek: Napheesa Collier, sidelined since September after tearing three ligaments in her left ankle, will make her 2026 season debut Wednesday against Seattle on a minutes restriction. Coach Cheryl Reeves cautioned that on-court expectations should be 'tempered' and 'this is not a full tilt,' despite Collier's 50-40-90 season last year.
author: The GoCheckMySports Desk
key_fact: Five-time All-Star Napheesa Collier returns Wednesday against Seattle under a minutes restriction following surgeries on both ankles, with the Lynx leading the league at 21-6.
bottom_line: Watch whether Collier's minutes restriction tightens or expands after Wednesday, and whether the Lynx lean on her more heavily in the All-Star break before the playoff push. If Collier cannot build on-court minutes or reports soreness in either ankle, the timetable for her full return resets.
body: Minnesota's 21-6 Lynx will have five-time All-Star Napheesa Collier back on Wednesday against Seattle, her first game since September when she tore three ligaments in her left ankle during Game 3 of the WNBA semifinals. Collier also underwent surgery on her right ankle on New Year's Day, according to ESPN's reporting. She was initially ruled out for four to six months following the two procedures.

Collier became the second player in WNBA history to shoot 50 percent from the floor, 40 percent from three and 90 percent from the free throw line in a single season last year, averaging 22.9 points, 7.3 rebounds, 3.2 assists, 1.6 steals and 1.5 blocks per game. She was among the front-runners for league MVP before a right ankle injury in August sidelined her for three weeks.

The All-Star returned to team practices on July 1 and has spent the last month ramping up conditioning and activities, per ESPN. Coach Cheryl Reeves set the last week as a target window for Collier's return, and the medical staff and Collier decided they were comfortable with her returning to game action. However, Reeves explicitly cautioned that on-court expectations should be "tempered" and "this is not a full tilt." She added that "there will come a time when I push her" to act like the best player, implying Collier is not expected to perform at that level immediately.

Collier will operate under a minutes restriction on Wednesday, though the brief does not specify the cap. Reeves noted "there are a couple more steps to go" in Collier's recovery before she reaches full capacity. The decision to activate her comes with Wednesday being Minnesota's last game before the All-Star break, with no play again until July 28.

The Lynx have already exceeded playoff expectations without Collier, anchored in part by standout rookie Olivia Miles, according to ESPN. Collier's absence from September through July represents approximately 10 months away from game competition, raising conditioning and rust concerns despite the month-long ramp-up since early July.
